name: Guru Rinpoche
mantraText: Om Ah Hum Vajra Guru Padma Siddhi Hum
openingText:
The Seven Line Prayer to Guru Rinpoche

 ཧཱུྂ ཨོ་རྒྱན་ཡུལ་གྱི་ནུབ་བྱང་མཚམས
Hung Orgyen yul gyi nub jang tsam
In the northwest of the land of Oddiyana

 པདྨ་གེ་སར་སྡོང་པོ་ལ
Pema gesar dongpo la
On the pistils of a lotus flower stem

 ཡ་མཚན་མཆོག་གི་དངོས་གྲུབ་བརྙེས
Yatsen chok gi ngodrub nyey
You attained the most marvelous supreme accomplishment

 པདྨ་འབྱུང་གནས་ཞེས་སུ་གྲགས
Pema jungney shyey su drag
You are renowned as the Lotus-Born

 འཁོར་དུ་མཁའ་འགྲོ་མང་པོས་བསྐོར
Khor du khandro mangpö kor
Surrounded by a retinue of many dakinis

 ཁྱེད་ཀྱི་རྗེས་སུ་བདག་བསྒྲུབ་ཀྱི
Khyey kyi jesu dak drub kyi
Following in your footsteps I will practice

 བྱིན་གྱིས་བརླབ་ཕྱིར་གཤེགས་སུ་གསོལ
Jingyi lab chir sheg su sol
Please approach to bestow your blessings

 གུ་རུ་པདྨ་སིདྡྷི་ཧཱུྂ
Guru Pema Siddhi Hung

~ Recite this prayer three times before beginning your practice ~
closingText:
དགེ་བ་འདི་ཡིས་མྱུར་དུ་བདག
Gé wa di yi nyur du dag
By this merit, may I swiftly

ཨོ་རྒྱན་གུ་རུ་འགྲུབ་གྱུར་ནས།
Or gyen gu ru drub gyur né
Attain the state of Orgyen Guru

འགྲོ་བ་གཅིག་ཀྱང་མ་ལུས་པ།
Dro wa chik kyang ma lü pa
And may every single living being without exception

དེ་དག་ས་ལ་འགོད་པར་ཤོག།
Dé dak sa la gö par shog
Be established in that very same level.
deityInfo:
GURU RINPOCHE (Padmasambhava) - The Second Buddha

Significance:
Guru Rinpoche, also known as Padmasambhava ('Lotus-Born'), is the founder of Tibetan Buddhism in the 8th century. He is revered as the 'Second Buddha' who tamed the negative forces of Tibet and established the Dharma there.

Historical Background:
Invited by King Trisong Detsen to subdue demons opposing the construction of Samye Monastery (the first Buddhist monastery in Tibet), Guru Rinpoche used his miraculous powers to pacify all obstacles.

Key Qualities:
• Wisdom and compassion united
• Power over all phenomena
• Ability to subdue negative forces
• Mastery of all teachings
• Embodiment of all three kayas (bodies of a Buddha)

The Mantra: OM AH HUM VAJRA GURU PADMA SIDDHI HUM
This powerful mantra contains the essence of Guru Rinpoche:
• OM - Body of all Buddhas
• AH - Speech of all Buddhas
• HUM - Mind of all Buddhas
• VAJRA - Vajra power (indestructible)
• GURU - Spiritual teacher
• PADMA - Lotus-born (his name)
• SIDDHI - Spiritual realizations
• HUM - May it be accomplished

Eight Manifestations:
Guru Rinpoche appeared in eight forms to benefit beings:
1. Padmasambhava - The Lotus-Born
2. Shakya Sengge - Lion of the Shakyas
3. Sengge Dradog - Lion's Roar
4. Loden Chokse - Great Wisdom
5. Dorje Drolod - Vajra Wrath
6. Padma Gyalpo - Lotus King
7. Nyima Ozer - Sun Rays
8. Pema Jungne - Lotus Source

Benefits of Practice:
• Removes obstacles on the spiritual path
• Receives blessings and protection
• Develops devotion and realization
• Purifies broken samayas (vows)
• Gains mastery over life and death

His Promise:
"To those who supplicate me, I will come. To those who have devotion, I will remain at their door. To those who remember me, I will be with them day and night."

stages.name: Main Practice
stages.mantraText: Om Ah Hum Vajra Guru Padma Siddhi Hum
